title: NCUA Credit Union Monitor
subtitle: National Credit Union Administration — enforcement, guidance, and rulemaking
schedule_note: Updated every 6 hours via GitHub Actions

items.title: FTC Takes Action Against XYZ Financial for Deceptive Lending Practices
items.summary: The Federal Trade Commission today announced enforcement action against XYZ Financial Corp, imposing a $12 million civil penalty for violations of the FTC Act related to deceptive consumer lending practices.
entities: Deceptive Lending Practices The Federal Trade Commission

items.title: DOJ and State AGs Announce $800M Settlement with Prescription Drug Distributor
items.summary: The Department of Justice and a coalition of 38 state attorneys general announced an $800 million settlement with Meridian Drug Distribution over its role in the opioid crisis.

items.title: FDIC Issues Guidance on Bank Merger Review Standards
items.summary: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation released final guidance updating its bank merger review process, clarifying standards for evaluating financial stability, competitive effects, and community reinvestment.
entities: Bank Merger Review Standards The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation

items.title: OCC Issues Final Rule on Climate-Related Financial Risk Management
items.summary: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency published a final rule requiring large national banks to establish climate-related financial risk management frameworks.
entities: Related Financial Risk Management The Office

items.title: State Banking Regulator Orders Community Bank to Improve BSA/AML Controls
items.summary: The state department of financial institutions issued a consent order requiring First Valley Community Bank to remediate deficiencies in its Bank Secrecy Act and anti-money laundering compliance program.
entities: State Banking Regulator Orders Community Bank

items.title: CFPB Proposes Rule to Limit Credit Card Late Fees
items.summary: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau issued a proposed rule that would cap credit card late fees at $8 for most issuers, down from the current safe harbor of $30. The proposed regulation would amend Regulation Z under the Truth in Lending Act.
entities: Limit Credit Card Late Fees The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau

items.title: SEC Charges Investment Adviser with Fraud and Breach of Fiduciary Duty
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission filed charges against Apex Capital Management LLC and its principal for orchestrating a $45 million fraud scheme.
